name: Umm al-Hasan bint Ali
title: Mourning Sister
shortDescription: Another daughter of Amir al-Mu'minin (AS) who was present in Karbala and taken captive with other Hashemite women.
fullStory:
Umm al-Hasan bint Ali ibn Abi Talib was another daughter of Amir al-Mu'minin (AS) and sister of the Master of Martyrs (AS), whose name is mentioned among the Karbala captives in some historical sources. Her mother was Umm Sa'id al-Thaqafi.

Alongside her sisters Zaynab (AS) and Umm Kulthum, she accompanied her brother's caravan from Medina to Karbala. On Ashura, like a firm mountain, Umm al-Hasan bore the grief of losing her brothers and loved ones.

On the afternoon of Ashura, when the tents were set on fire, she acted as a shield for the young children and was then sent towards Kufa with bound hands along with the others.

History records that when the crushing sermons of Zaynab (AS) and Imam Sajjad (AS) were delivered in Kufa and Sham, women like Umm al-Hasan also awakened public opinion with their wails and tears.
quote: (Condition of Ahl al-Bayt women:) O Muhammad! Your daughters are taken as captives...
